module_mood_contract: earned trust — 'the way I work with agents is something my staff engineer and my CISO can sign off on, before I even try anything big'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances with contrast.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Subagent-vs-main-thread LO satisfied by Ex1+Ex2 (curated skills as subagents) vs Ex3 (test-strategy authoring in main thread). Contrast is the pedagogy; both instances present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All five M3 forcing functions (disclose-weakest-part, one-question-at-a-time, name-the-delta, pick-the-threat+write-ADR, invoke-on-feature) live inside registry-expanded prompt markers (lines 53, 61, 65) or inside referenced exercise files, not in body prose around them.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No pair-share, tell-the-room, or comparison prompt constructions found. Line 26 ('The room's features will differ wildly; that's the point') is a Connections carve-out — contextualising why the question was asked, not authoring cohort management.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Task-selection floor + ceiling both explicit.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 8: 'Not a typo-fix, not a quarter-long epic' names both floor and ceiling. Lines 75-77: Bring-to-Module-4 repeats the same callout with stakes framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 123-127: three named-verb falsifiable outcomes with explicit falsifiability criteria for each.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 128-135: three contract rows (ADR, test-strategy SKILL.md, CLAUDE.local.md) with stable identifier, producing prompt, and consuming modules named.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 137-146: six rows covering Ex1 (passivity), Ex2 (menu-shopping, missing ADR), Ex3 (question-dump, default-acceptance, invocation skip), and Debrief (generic rule). Each row names dominant failure + escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Convenience prompts with known shape-bias get body callout.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 57-58 (post-prompt callout after ae101-m3-sharpen-skill): names same-context self-audit charity bias + three opt-up alternatives (over-flag, no-preamble, fresh-session). Behaviour-class REVISE verdict for this shape treated as accepted-with-mitigation per rule 51.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 60-63 (Clear the session pre-action prose): 'The agent converges: anything still in scrollback that should have landed in the skill or rules-file, anything you flagged as team-worthy...' names the convergence targets before ae101-m3-ready-to-clear runs. The prompt itself is just 'Ready to clear? All learnings in?' — open. Pre-action prose previews what the prompt will surface. Pre-existing finding from prior audit (noted as non-blocking, load_bearing false).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Trim 'The agent converges: anything...' enumeration to a one-line transition ('Signal the wrap-up; the agent converges what's still loose.') — preview the move without listing the outputs.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 51/53 (sharpen): 'Ask Claude to sharpen...'; line 59/61 (ready-to-clear): 'If the agent names something missed...'; line 63/65 (clear): action lead-in preceding /clear marker. All within 20 words.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in the registry; module files reference by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 53, 61, 65: three registry markers (ae101-m3-sharpen-skill, ae101-m3-ready-to-clear, ae101-m3-clear-session) verified present in curriculum/prompts/. All three registry files confirmed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer-block mood target (line 90): 'earned trust — the way I work with agents is something my staff engineer and my CISO can sign off on, before I even try anything big.' Trace: teaching beats 8-9 throughout; close 9. Mood contract honoured.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (line 4): 'earn your staff engineer's and CISO's trust on a small piece you're shipping' — positive discipline. Key Concepts bullets lead with 'curated skill is upstream work', 'access surface is what STRIDE chews on', 'test-strategy skill authored on your codebase is worth more' — all discipline-first.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity: no front-running downstream modules.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Subagent primitive introduced here per strategy. plan.md, verifier, three-pattern (M5 territory) not named. Reference links to subagents+skills in Prework are marked Optional and scoped to existing reference material.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 39-47: Theme 1 (90% correct) via 'last 10% is yours'; Theme 3 (mirror) explicitly ('the mirror reflects what your own read would miss'); Theme 5 (capture your world) via 'your codebase teaches the skill, not the manual'. Three of six themes surfaced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts breadcrumb shape: gates 3/4/5 run first.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Gates checked in order: rule 3 (discipline-first on all bullets — PASS); rule 4 (no front-running — PASS); rule 5 (themes present — PASS). Breadcrumbs on survivors use correct bold-Remember format. No standalone Themes section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; vocabulary lectures land mid-module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Skills-from-the-frontier lecture introduces vocabulary (curated/authored, subagent/main-thread) before Exercises 1-3 that require that vocabulary. Placed after open-the-side-quest setup, before exercises — correct mid-module vocabulary placement per rule 1.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands 8+ at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching beats: Connections 8, Lecture 8, Ex1+Ex2 9, Ex3 8, Sharpen 8, Bring-to-M4 8. Close 9. Setup/transition beats exempt (mood_score null): Prework, Open-the-side-quest, Clear session. No teaching beat below 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early; M3 earned-trust must not bleed into M4 curious readiness.
rules_evaluated.evidence: /clear is a hard session boundary (line 65). Bring-to-Module-4 creates forward tension (task naming) not resolution. M4's curious readiness is deferred to M4's open.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across persona variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Disclose-weakest-part and invoke-on-feature forcing functions live inside registry prompts the speed-runner copies. Teaching moment (real architectural call under named pressure) lands at Ex2 ADR write which is structurally required. Persona trace: mood 9 at Ex1+Ex2 teaching peak.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — strategy doc M3 Big Idea honoured.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy M3: 'two curated skills used, one authored skill shipped; team-kit accretion seeded.' Module Big Idea (line 4) matches verbatim in spirit. Module close (line 68): 'Two signatures, earned.' Discipline installed before M5 send-off per strategy.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: M3 close hands off to M4 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 69-73 (Next): explicitly names M4's three-block frame and how M3 artifacts (ADR, test-strategy skill, quality criteria) feed M4's walk-and-fill. Lines 72-77 (Bring-to-M4): task-sizing task for M4's curious-readiness open. Matches strategy M4 open: 'You arrive with one or two candidates you've surfaced yourself.'
